# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.210.0/http/server.ts";

const DEFAULT_MODULES = [
  "https://deno.land/std@0.210.0/fmt/colors.ts",
  // Add more default modules here if needed
];

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const params = url.searchParams;

  // Get module paths from query parameters
  const queryModules = params.getAll("module");

  // Get module paths from environment variables
  const envModules = Deno.env.get("MODULES");
  const envModulePaths = envModules ? envModules.split(",").map(s => s.trim()).filter(s => s.length > 0) : [];

  // Combine all module paths, prioritizing query parameters, then environment variables, then defaults
  const allModulePaths = [...queryModules, ...envModulePaths, ...DEFAULT_MODULES];

  const loadedModules: { [key: string]: any } = {};
  const errors: { [key: string]: string } = {};

  for (const modulePath of allModulePaths) {
    try {
      // Dynamic import
      const module = await import(modulePath);
      // Use the modulePath as the key, or derive a more friendly name if needed
      loadedModules[modulePath] = module;
    } catch (e) {
      console.error(`Failed to load module ${modulePath}:`, e);
      errors[modulePath] = e instanceof Error ? e.message : String(e);
    }
  }

  // Example of using a loaded module (if 'colors' was loaded)
  let outputMessage = "Modules loaded successfully!";
  if (loadedModules["https://deno.land/std@0.210.0/fmt/colors.ts"]) {
    const colors = loadedModules["https://deno.land/std@0.210.0/fmt/colors.ts"];
    outputMessage = colors.green(outputMessage);
  }

  // Prepare response
  const responseBody = {
    message: outputMessage,
    loaded: Object.keys(loadedModules),
    failed: errors,
    details: {
      loadedModules: Object.fromEntries(
        Object.entries(loadedModules).map(([path, mod]) => [
          path,
          // Only expose a few properties for demonstration, avoid exposing entire module objects directly
          Object.keys(mod).filter(key => typeof mod[key] === 'function' || typeof mod[key] === 'string')
        ])
      ),
      errors: errors,
    }
  };

  return new Response(JSON.stringify(responseBody, null, 2), {
    headers: { "content-type": "application/json" },
  });
}

serve(handler);
